requirement: Stable schema version required before React or other consumers read dashboard data.
Generation time must identify when the read-only snapshot was produced.
Each generated snapshot must expose a producer-owned identity so browser refresh can detect valid changes without inventing state.
Each generated snapshot must expose a producer-owned content identity for read-only live refresh and last-known-good comparison.
Source files must identify existing TSV, Markdown, policy, and settings files using relative paths without raw logs, remote URLs, or unnecessary absolute paths.
Source commands must identify existing read-only commands used to build the snapshot without requiring prose parsing.
Non-fatal conditions must be visible without failing the whole snapshot.
Selected context must bind the active menu, workflow context, repository, current step, evidence, blockers, and next safe action before the UI presents context-specific live state.
Selected context menu id must come from producer-owned menu or workflow state rather than browser inference.
Selected context workflow context must be passed to product authority, Git, CI, Security, and evidence readers without fixed product-improvement assumptions.
Selected context target repository must identify the current repository scope without making missing product repositories block lesson-only progress.
Selected context target repository name must be safe display text derived from existing settings or product authority.
Selected context repository path state must avoid leaking unnecessary absolute paths while distinguishing missing product repositories.
Selected context product type must come from product manifests or policy data rather than UI stack-specific branches.
Selected context product Git usage mode must come from product workflow policy/settings so the UI does not infer whether Git or CI is required.
Selected context Git requirement must expose whether Git is required for the selected product workflow mode.
Selected context CI requirement must expose whether CI is required for the selected product workflow mode.
Selected context current step id must remain a producer-owned diagnostic key and not replace learner-facing display labels.
Selected context current step label must be producer-owned display data so the UI does not invent step labels from screenshots.
Selected context current step index must be producer-owned for current-step summaries and progress strips.
Selected context current step total must be producer-owned for current-step summaries and progress strips.
Selected context update time must be producer-owned so page headers can show freshness without browser inference.
Selected context Git status must be read from existing evidence/settings or explicitly not_applicable when product Git usage mode does not require Git.
Selected context CI status must be read from existing evidence or explicitly not_applicable when product Git usage mode does not require CI.
Selected context Security status must be read from policy or evidence and must preserve approval and blocker boundaries.
Selected context evidence status must preserve not-run and stale states instead of collapsing them into unknown.
Selected context next safe action must be producer-owned display data and must not execute commands.
Selected context blockers must contain current-context blockers and stay separate from optional manual follow-ups.
Each displayed menu context must have complete producer-owned context data so the browser never switches to an invented or thin context.
Each contexts_by_menu entry must use the same structure and validation rules as selected_context.
The context map key and the entry menu id must identify the same selectable menu context.
Context map workflow context must be producer-owned for every selectable menu.
Context map repository data must be complete enough for page headers, menu strips, and Safety scope display.
Context map product Git usage mode must be complete for every selectable menu.
Context map Git requirement must be complete for every selectable menu.
Context map CI requirement must be complete for every selectable menu.
Context map current step id must come from lesson state, workflow context map, or workflow evidence.
Context map Git status must be producer-owned or explicitly manual_required/not_run/stale/not_applicable.
Context map CI status must be producer-owned or explicitly manual_required/not_run/stale/not_applicable.
Context map Security status must be producer-owned or explicitly manual_required/not_run/stale.
Context map evidence status must preserve blocked, stale, not_run, and manual_required states.
Context map blockers must be scoped to the current menu and must not mix lesson-only progress with product-operation blockers unless the context requires product operations.
Available contexts must list menu choices the browser may display without inventing workflow or repository state.
Available context menu ids must use the same vocabulary as selected_context.menu_id.
Available context workflow contexts must let the UI switch views without hard-coded product context branches.
Available context target repository names must be safe display text and may be not applicable for lesson-repository contexts.
Available context status must summarize whether the context can be inspected without treating optional or not-run evidence as healthy.
Available context selectability must be producer-owned so the browser does not activate menus with missing lesson state or missing target repositories.
Unavailable context reason keys must identify why the menu cannot become the active dashboard context.
Unavailable context details must explain the missing state without exposing raw paths, logs, or secrets.
Unavailable context next actions must name the safe check or review needed before activation.
Repository selection must expose registry-backed candidates and display-only selection commands without giving the browser direct write authority.
Repository selection status must say whether the selected product repository can be trusted, is missing, needs manual selection, or does not apply.
Repository selection menu id must match the selected dashboard context without hard-coded product names.
Repository selection workflow context must come from the same producer-owned context map as selected_context.
Repository selection current id must come from PRODUCT_REPOSITORY_SELECTION.tsv, an explicit request override, or an explicit not-selected state.
Repository selection current name must be safe display text from the registry or not_selected/not_applicable.
Repository selection state must distinguish saved selection, fallback, request override, and unavailable registry contexts.
Repository selection registry source must be exposed as a safe relative path.
Repository selection saved-selection source must be exposed as a safe relative path.
Repository selection options must list eligible registry rows for the selected menu without leaking raw local paths.
Repository selection option id must be a registry id and must not depend on basename-only inference.
Repository selection option display name must be safe text from the registry or product profile.
Repository selection option primary menu must come from the registry row.
Repository selection option allowed contexts must drive menu eligibility instead of hard-coded product branches.
Repository selection option product type must use the generic product type vocabulary.
Repository selection option source must distinguish how the registry row was created without using fixture-specific values.
Repository selection option path state must summarize parent-side filesystem observation without displaying raw local paths.
Repository selection option Git state must reflect whether Git metadata is required and present.
Repository selection option status must say whether this candidate can be safely selected.
Repository selection option selectability must be producer-owned so the UI does not enable invalid candidates.
Repository selection option selected flag must identify the current candidate without browser inference.
Repository selection option reason key must explain unavailable candidates without repeated generic copy.
Repository selection option disabled detail must explain missing evidence without raw paths or logs.
Repository selection command must be display-only and must use the guarded product-repository-registry CLI.
Each partial failure must identify the unavailable or optional source.
Each partial failure must use the fixed status vocabulary subset for true failed, blocked, or unknown failure conditions.
Each partial failure must explain why the source was unavailable without raw logs or secrets.
Each partial failure may identify the manual follow-up command needed for authoritative validation.
The home summary must classify the current control-center context without making it a new source of truth.
The dashboard UI language decision must expose the selected learning/workflow display language from existing lesson settings.
The dashboard must expose the selected workflow display locale so browser UI labels can follow Settings rather than the browser locale alone.
The React UI must receive the supported Dashboard locale resolved from the workflow display language.
The React UI must receive the text direction for the resolved Dashboard locale so Arabic can render right-to-left while technical values stay isolated.
The dashboard must expose the selected display depth so the browser can adjust explanation density and technical disclosure without changing workflow authority.
The snapshot must explain the next safe action without executing it.
The overview primary action must be structured so the UI can render a compact next action without parsing prose.
The overview primary action must include a concise explanation for the suggested review action.
The overview primary action must identify the target surface or workflow without executing anything.
The overview primary action must identify the expected safe result in display-only text.
The overview primary action must expose risk as structured data.
The overview primary action must expose state as structured data.
The overview primary action must identify the source field or decision path that produced it.
Overview metric must be producer-owned so the browser does not invent overview progress.
Lesson metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code lesson progress.
Workflow metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code workflow progress.
Maintenance metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code maintenance progress.
Security metric must be producer-owned so health cards and Explore Pages do not hard-code security progress.
Category metrics must include a total count.
Category metrics must include a healthy count.
Category metrics must include a warning count.
Category metrics must include a problem count.
Category metrics must include a producer-owned percentage for progress rings and Explore Pages.
Category metrics must include a unit label such as items, steps, or checks.
Category metrics must expose state as structured data.
Each concise guidance item must identify whether it belongs to the lesson surface or workflow surface.
Guidance must distinguish plain-language learner help from practical engineering workflow advice where needed.
Guidance must classify whether the point is informational, a caution, or a suggested next action.
Guidance messages must stay concise and safe for display without raw logs, secrets, or external payloads.
Guidance may point to an existing dashboard or check command without executing it.
Each blocker must identify the required source or gate that prevents safe progress.
Required blockers must stay separate from optional partial failures.
Each blocker must explain the condition without raw logs or secrets.
Each blocker may identify the manual follow-up command needed to unblock progress.
Each manual follow-up must identify an optional, cached, or unknown check without classifying it as a true partial failure.
Manual follow-ups must use a fixed non-authoritative status vocabulary.
Each manual follow-up must explain the check without raw logs or secrets.
Each manual follow-up may identify the manual command needed for authoritative validation.
Live status must be a fast producer-owned payload for the dashboard operational cards, separate from the slower snapshot payload.
Live status schema version must identify the fast payload contract independently from dashboard snapshots.
Live status generated_at must identify when the live payload was generated, not a fabricated operation time.
Live status menu_id must match the selected dashboard menu request.
Live status workflow_context must identify the selected workflow scope.
Live status target_repository must describe the repository that every operational card is observing.
Live status target repository name must be display-safe and must not be inferred by React from stale lesson defaults.
Live status path_state must report whether the selected target path exists.
Live status git_state must report whether the selected target has Git metadata when Git is relevant.
Live status git_usage_mode must come from selected workflow Git policy.
Live status repository_state must expose the current selected repository state used by Git and CI decisions.
Live status branch must be the currently observed selected repository branch or blank when unavailable.
Live status head must be the observed selected repository head revision or blank when unavailable.
Live status upstream must be the observed upstream branch or blank when unavailable.
Live status dirty_count must expose uncommitted tracked changes for the selected repository.
Live status untracked_count must expose untracked files for the selected repository.
Live status ahead must expose commits ahead of upstream for the selected repository.
Live status behind must expose commits behind upstream for the selected repository.
Live status checks must contain the four overview operational cards and no UI-invented status.
Local test check must describe the latest or most urgent local verification result for the selected repository.
Git sync check must describe the selected repository commit, push, upstream, and local/remote sync state.
CI check must describe the currently observed CI run or the exact reason it cannot be observed.
Security check must describe the currently observed safety gate, blocker, approval, or missing evidence.
Each live check must expose a shared structured status.
Each live check must identify when that check was actually observed, or not_collected when no evidence exists.
Each live check must expose a stable detail code for tests and localized explanation.
Each live check must identify the producer-owned evidence source.
Each live check must provide a short fallback current-position summary for the overview card.
Each live check must explain what concrete evidence or missing condition produced the status.
Each live check must explain the next safe place to inspect or action to consider without executing browser operations.
Each live check must route its detailed explanation to an existing dashboard page.
Each live check must expose evidence freshness separately from pass/fail status.
Each live check must expose whether the evidence is authoritative, advisory, manually required, or absent.
Each live check must expose the operational risk level of acting on the current state.
Each live check may expose a display-only command preview or not_applicable.
Each live check must identify the item that drives the overview card summary.
Each live check may include detailed items for the popup and detail pages.
Each live check item must expose a stable source id.
Each live check item must classify the operational area without UI string parsing.
Each live check item must classify the check kind without UI string parsing.
Each live check item must preserve structured status.
Each live check item must identify its own observation time or not_collected.
Each live check item must expose item-level freshness.
Each live check item must expose item-level authority.
Each live check item must provide a concise fallback label for details.
Each live check item may identify safe source artifacts without raw logs or secrets.
Each live check item may expose a display-only command preview or not_applicable.
Each live check item must expose item-level blocker count.
CI live check may expose the observed workflow name.
CI live check may expose the observed provider run status.
CI live check may expose the observed provider conclusion.
CI live check may expose the observed provider run id.
CI live check may expose the observed provider run URL for display-only navigation.
CI live check must expose the selected repository head that the CI match decision is based on.
CI live check may expose the observed CI run head sha.
CI live check may expose the observed CI run head branch.
CI live check must state whether the observed run head matches the selected repository head.
STEP 1-7 data must come from existing lesson state and settings sources.
STEP 1-7 concise lesson points must come from structured lesson state/settings, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-7 lesson warnings must come from structured missing/unknown state, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-7 next learning action must be a read-only explanation derived from structured lesson state/settings.
STEP 1-14 data must come from existing lesson14 state and settings sources.
STEP 1-14 concise lesson points must come from structured lesson state/settings, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-14 lesson warnings must come from structured missing/unknown state, not CLI prose.
STEP 1-14 next learning action must be a read-only explanation derived from structured lesson state/settings.
Applied lesson data must reuse existing menu prerequisite and team-development status sources.
Applied lesson concise points must come from structured menu prerequisite state, not CLI prose.
Applied lesson warnings must come from structured missing/approval state, not CLI prose.
Applied lesson next learning action must be a read-only explanation derived from structured menu prerequisite state.
Product repository state must come from existing repository-boundary and product workflow sources.
Product repository configured name must be emitted by the producer without exposing absolute paths.
Product repository workflow context must identify which selected menu scope the repository status describes.
Product repository path state must report whether the configured product path exists separately from Git requirements.
Product repository Git state must report whether a Git worktree exists separately from product path existence.
Product repository Git requirement must come from product workflow Git usage policy for the selected menu context.
Product repository CI requirement must come from product workflow Git usage policy for the selected menu context.
Product document state must come from existing document and workflow-pair checks.
Documents page data must be producer-owned so React does not keep a fixed document array.
Documents page status must summarize the producer-owned documents catalog without browser inference.
Documents page groups must identify learner intent and display order from dashboard data.
Each document group must expose a stable id.
Each document group must expose a localization key for the primary label.
Each document group must expose a localization key for the group explanation.
Each document group must expose producer-owned display order.
Documents page catalog must list document cards from producer-owned data.
Each document catalog item must expose a stable id.
Each document catalog item must reference a known documents group.
Each document catalog item must expose a stable document role.
Each document catalog item must expose a safe relative path.
Each document catalog item must expose structured readiness or review state.
Each document catalog item must identify which producer source determined its status.
Each document catalog item must classify its intended audience without hard-coded UI branches.
Each document catalog item must expose producer-owned display order.
Each document catalog item must route related operational checks to an existing dashboard page.
Documents page must expose read-only reference commands for existing document guidance tools without browser execution.
Each related document command must expose a stable id.
Each related document command must expose a localization key for the primary label.
Each related document command must expose a localization key for the description.
Each related document command must expose a safe display-only command string.
Each related document command must expose producer-owned display order.
Settings page data must be producer-owned so React does not keep fixed setting cards or infer current setting values.
Settings page status must summarize the producer-owned settings catalog without browser inference.
Settings groups must organize context, dashboard, learning, workflow, and security settings from producer-owned data.
Each settings group must expose a stable id.
Each settings group must expose a localization key for the primary label.
Each settings group must expose a localization key for the group explanation.
Each settings group must expose a structured readiness or review state.
Each settings group must expose producer-owned display order.
Settings items must expose current values, source files, changeability, approval needs, and update metadata for the guarded Settings mutation boundary.
Each settings item must expose a stable id.
Each settings item must reference a known settings group.
Each settings item must expose the settings scope without relying on route-specific UI branches.
Each settings item must expose a localization key for the item label.
Each settings item must expose a localization key for the item explanation.
Each settings item must expose the current machine-readable value as display-safe text.
Each settings item must expose a producer-owned fallback current value label.
Each settings item must expose structured readiness or review state without collapsing approval-required states.
Each settings item must identify the safe relative source file that owns the current value.
Each editable settings item must expose allowed values from existing policy or setting readers for update validation.
Editable settings must be limited to the guarded repo-local Settings tool and must not imply arbitrary browser writes.
Reviewable items may open a review dialog; editable items must also support Settings plan/apply validation before writes.
Each settings item must classify the risk of changing the setting before Settings plan/apply may proceed.
Each settings item must state whether a future change requires explicit confirmation.
Each settings item must expose producer-owned consistency status, reason, affected setting ids, and next safe action so React does not infer policy conflicts.
Each settings consistency object must expose the row-level policy state using the shared state vocabulary.
Each settings consistency object must classify whether the condition is informational, compatibility warning, or write-blocking error.
Each settings consistency object must expose a stable reason code for tests and future clients.
Each settings consistency object must expose a localization key for the human-readable reason.
Each settings consistency object must expose a localization key for the safe next action.
Each settings consistency object may expose the effective mode or current setting value without raw logs or command output.
Each settings consistency object must identify related setting ids so UI and tests can show cross-setting conflicts without hard-coded text parsing.
Each settings item must explain whether it is editable through Settings or why mutation is unavailable.
Each settings item must route related checks to an existing dashboard page.
Each settings item must expose a stable action id for Settings review and allowlisted update routing.
Each settings item must expose producer-owned review metadata so the modal does not invent impact or validation text.
Each settings item review must expose a localization key for the expected impact.
Each settings item review must identify the target file the guarded Settings writer updates or reviews.
Each settings item review must expose the validation state that supports or blocks a future change.
Each settings item review must expose a localization key for the safe update explanation.
Product Git sync state must remain separate from Git policy readiness and may be not_applicable when the selected product workflow does not use Git.
Product CI state must be optional, evidence-backed, missing, not_run, or not_applicable unless a required check is requested.
Git operation rows must describe Push, PR, CI, sync, and Merge settings from producer-owned workflow data.
Top-level operational decision must be producer-owned so the overview and detail pages do not infer readiness from labels.
Operational decision status must use the shared state vocabulary.
Operational decision question must state the user-facing decision being answered.
Primary blocker source must point to the owner source that most directly controls the decision.
Operational decision reason must explain the top blocker or current readiness condition in plain language.
Operational decision next safe action must be display-only and must not execute commands.
Operational decision done condition must say when the user can treat the state as complete.
Operational decision approval boundary must preserve Git, CI, merge, cleanup, credential, and product-write safety limits.
Operational decision risk level must classify the current judgment for non-engineer and junior-engineer review.
Operational decision freshness must show whether the decision is current enough for use.
Operational decision authority must separate stored proof, manual review, advisory lookup, and missing collection.
Operational decision source id must be stable for tests and detail navigation.
Operational decision must include plain-language non-engineer guidance.
Operational decision must include junior-engineer guidance without replacing non-engineer guidance.
Operational decision actions must remain display-only.
Each primary Control Center page must have a producer-owned decision contract.
Decision page id must identify an existing primary page.
Decision page title must be safe display text.
Decision page scope must identify selected repository, context, lesson, or workflow scope.
Decision pages must serve non-engineers and junior engineers.
Decision page status must use the shared state vocabulary.
Decision page must state the question answered by the page.
Decision page current judgment must explain if work can continue, needs review, is blocked, or requires approval.
Decision page top reason must give the most important evidence gap or blocker.
Decision page evidence confidence must expose authority and freshness without UI inference.
Decision page must-review points must identify the first evidence users should inspect.
Decision page next action must be display-only and scoped to an existing page or manual command.
Decision page detail target must route to an existing Control Center page.
Decision page owner source must not be ui.
Decision page source id must link back to the producer source.
Decision page authority must use the evidence authority vocabulary.
Decision page freshness must use the evidence freshness vocabulary.
Decision page risk level must classify the user-facing decision.
Decision page actions must remain display-only.
Repository changes must summarize branch, worktree, and sync state without leaking absolute paths.
Repository change status must use the shared state vocabulary.
Repository change observation time must identify when Git state was read.
Repository change path state must come from the selected repository resolver.
Repository change Git state must show whether Git metadata is available.
Repository change branch must be safe display text and may be empty for detached HEAD.
Repository change head must be a short display-safe revision.
Repository change upstream must be display-safe and may be empty when not configured.
Repository change main target must identify main or origin/main when available.
Repository change detached flag must show detached HEAD without parsing branch labels.
Repository change staged count must be non-negative.
Repository change unstaged count must be non-negative.
Repository change untracked count must be non-negative.
Repository change ahead count must be non-negative.
Repository change behind count must be non-negative.
Repository change worktree count must be non-negative.
Repository change role counts must separate staged, unstaged, and untracked counts.
Repository change samples must stay display-safe and bounded.
Repository change stale reason must explain why the state is not current.
Repository development workflow state must expose phase and runner-record freshness as advisory owner data.
Repository development current phase must use the workflow phase vocabulary.
Repository development phase order must match the workflow policy order.
Repository development inference reason must explain why the phase was selected.
Repository development purpose must come from the workflow policy.
Repository development required inputs must come from the workflow policy.
Repository development allowed writes must come from the workflow policy.
Repository development recommended checks must come from the workflow policy.
Repository development required checks must come from the workflow policy.
Repository development Git and CI expectations must come from the workflow policy.
Repository development approval boundary must come from the workflow policy.
Repository development cleanup behavior must preserve plan-only boundaries.
Repository development stop conditions must be visible without running the workflow.
Repository development runner records must be advisory and must not count as release proof.
Repository development source files must identify the policy sources.
Workflow evidence events must normalize recent workflow evidence for timeline and history views.
Workflow event id must be stable.
Workflow event source id must link to the owner evidence.
Workflow event observation time must be explicit.
Workflow event repository head must identify the observed repository revision when available.
Workflow event status must use the shared state vocabulary.
Workflow event freshness must use the evidence freshness vocabulary.
Workflow event authority must use the evidence authority vocabulary.
Workflow event artifact path must be safe and may be product-scoped.
Workflow event summary must be non-empty display text.
CI evidence roles must separate branch, PR, main, local tests, and provider visibility.
CI evidence role must identify which confidence signal is represented.
CI evidence role status must use the shared state vocabulary.
CI evidence role source id must link back to product evidence.
CI evidence role head match must show whether evidence is for the current head when known.
CI evidence role authority must separate proof from availability.
CI evidence role freshness must show if the evidence is usable for the current decision.
CI evidence role summary must be plain-language display text.
CI evidence role observation time must be explicit.
Each Git operation row must expose a stable id for reusable rendering.
Each Git operation row must expose a display label without relying on one screenshot language.
Each Git operation row must expose structured status.
Each Git operation row must expose the configured mode or gate label as safe text.
Each Git operation row must describe what the operation state means without executing the operation.
Current workflow evidence rows must be producer-owned instead of being inferred from Git operation settings.
Each current workflow evidence row must expose a stable id.
Each current workflow evidence row may expose producer-owned time, freshness text, or blank text when no real run time exists.
Each current workflow evidence row must expose the evidence type.
Each current workflow evidence row must expose target repository, branch, or PR scope without secrets.
Each current workflow evidence row must expose a concise evidence description.
Each current workflow evidence row must expose structured status.
Each current workflow evidence row may expose a display-only reference such as context, command, commit, or PR id.
Each current workflow evidence row must identify whether its reference describes selected context, Git sync, CI, product authority, or security gate evidence.
Each current workflow evidence row may expose a display-only check command for the row-specific next action.
Each current workflow evidence row must bind the reference to the selected menu or workflow context.
Product authority must summarize external product repository structure, manifest, evidence, and product-operation blockers without UI-invented judgment.
Product authority status must preserve not-run and stale evidence states.
Product repository status must identify the configured product repository without blocking lesson-only progress.
Product repository status must expose the configured product repository name in safe text.
Product repository blockers must be scoped to product operations rather than lesson-only progress.
Product summary must expose the learner-confirmed product display profile from the external repository so UI does not infer names from repository paths or prose.
Product summary status must expose whether ops/PRODUCT_PROFILE.json is present, valid, and displayable without inferring a fallback name.
Product summary name must come from ops/PRODUCT_PROFILE.json display_name, using the producer fallback order only when the UI has not selected a locale.
Product summary display_name must expose locale-keyed product names declared by the product display profile.
Product summary Japanese display name must preserve the learner-confirmed Japanese artifact name when available.
Product summary English display name must preserve the learner-confirmed English artifact name when available.
Product summary description may expose short locale-keyed profile descriptions without requiring the UI to parse product documents.
Product summary source_documents must list only safe relative documents that informed the recommendation or confirmation.
Product summary source path must identify ops/PRODUCT_PROFILE.json as the canonical external repository profile.
Product operation mode must expose parent-managed, standalone, reconnecting, or repair-required state from ops/PRODUCT_OPERATION_MODE.tsv without UI inference.
Product operation mode status must show whether product AGENTS.MD, legacy AGENT.md, and mode records are ready for product operations.
Product operation mode must preserve the product-declared workflow mode.
Product operation mode must expose whether parent/product rule connection is valid or intentionally not applicable.
Product operation mode must expose a concise repair reason when it is not ready.
Product operation mode must expose the next safe action without executing product changes.
Product repository file structure and roles must come from the external product repository's dedicated read-only index, not from dashboard-side filesystem scanning.
Product repository index status must show whether the external read-only index was available without becoming a product-operation gate blocker.
Product repository index path must identify the external repository-owned index file such as ops/REPOSITORY_INDEX.json.
Product repository index summary must expose producer-owned directory, file, and total counts for the external repository index.

Product repository index entries must include all non-hidden external repository directories and files except generated dependency directories; index-to-filesystem consistency belongs to the external repository main CI.
Product repository index roles must be supplied by the external repository so dashboard UI does not infer file responsibilities from paths.
Product authority must expose manifest coverage derived from product structure and ops manifests.
Missing required manifest or structure rows must be visible as source ids.
Missing optional manifest or structure rows must be visible without becoming blockers.
Product authority must expose latest evidence status and freshness for Git, docs, CI, security, approvals, and gates.
Each evidence summary item must use a stable source id.
Each evidence summary item must expose the workflow context where the evidence applies, or all when the evidence is context-neutral.
Each evidence summary item must preserve evidence status vocabulary.
Each evidence summary item must expose freshness separately from status.
Each evidence summary item must expose authority separately from status.
Each evidence summary item must expose whether the selected workflow context requires it so optional details do not block progress.
Each evidence summary item must expose its own observation time for detail pages and stale-state decisions.
Each evidence summary item must expose the maximum accepted age used for stale-state decisions.
Each evidence summary item must expose a sanitized product-root label without leaking unnecessary absolute paths.
Each evidence summary item must expose the product HEAD it applies to so stale evidence can be distinguished from current evidence.
Each evidence summary item must expose safe source artifacts without raw logs, secrets, or unnecessary absolute paths.
Each evidence summary item must expose source-scoped blockers so popups and detail pages can explain why progress is stopped.
Each evidence summary item may expose a display-only command preview without enabling browser execution.
Each evidence summary item must expose a stable detail code for localized explanation and row-specific help.
Each evidence summary item may identify the detail item driving the overview card summary.
Each evidence summary item may reference the product-local evidence detail manifest row that explains the check.
Each evidence summary item may reference a sanitized product evidence detail artifact path for details and history.
Each evidence summary item must provide a concise user-facing judgment summary when detail manifests are available.
Each evidence summary item must explain why that status was produced without relying on UI-side inference.
Each evidence summary item must explain the next safe action or inspection target without executing it.
Each evidence summary item must classify the operational risk of acting on the current state.
Product-operation blockers must stay separate from lesson-only summary blockers.
Each product-operation blocker must identify the product authority source id.
Each product-operation blocker must preserve source status without collapsing not-run or stale states.
Product-operation blockers may expose read-only command previews for manual follow-up.
Browser Debug handoff must expose Dashboard Control Center review package, agent ingest, and report readiness without making the dashboard execute Browser Debug CLI or provider APIs.
Browser Debug handoff schema version must let future consumers evolve the handoff contract without parsing UI text.
Browser Debug handoff status must summarize local evidence readiness while staying advisory for dashboard maintenance.
Browser Debug handoff target must identify the dashboard surface being reviewed.
Browser Debug handoff must expose whether the selected free-development repository provides Browser Debug CLI.
Browser Debug tool state must stay separate from review and agent artifact state.
Browser Debug tool status must preserve missing or manual selection states without mutating repository selection.
Browser Debug tool command must be display-only and scoped to product: paths rather than absolute local paths.
Browser Debug tool source must identify that the CLI comes from the selected free-development repository.
Browser Debug manifest state must identify the lesson-owned target manifest generator.
Browser Debug manifest status must show whether the lesson-owned manifest generator is present.
Browser Debug manifest path must stay inside this lesson repository.
Browser Debug manifest command must be a display-only local command preview.
Browser Debug review state must expose review artifact index collection without making the dashboard launch browsers.
Browser Debug review status must preserve not_run when no local review artifact exists.
Browser Debug review artifact path must be a safe relative artifact path or not_collected.
Browser Debug review command must be a display-only preview and must not be executed by the dashboard.
Browser Debug agent package state must expose subscription/API-neutral evidence packet readiness.
